summary: Reviewed the host-controller-tweaks spec and surrounding documentation changes; the actual host controller and display implementation files are not included in the pack. The direction is broadly coherent, but the spec contains one direct trigger-condition contradiction and several assumptions that cannot be validated against the live code from this pack. The main risk is implementers following the ambiguous rows and either omitting game 2+ briefings or accidentally changing the display rules panel.
appears_sound: The proposal preserves server-side call-delay behavior and only removes the host-facing hint, matching the project rule that call pacing stays server-enforced (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:32, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:42).

The first-game rules check is based on the minimum `game_index` in the session rather than assuming game index 1, which is more robust for reordered or renumbered sessions (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:74, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:126).

The proposed `getColourName()` helper is pure shared logic with a colocated native Node test including an invalid input case, which fits the repo's `src/lib/*.test.ts` testing pattern (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:105).

findings.description: The spec contradicts itself on whether the briefing renders for every zero-call game or only the first game. An implementer following the file-impact row would omit the pre-game prize briefing from games 2+, violating the stated workflow goal.
findings.evidence: The trigger section says the briefing renders whenever `numbers_called_count === 0` for every game, but the file-impact row says `game-control.tsx` should render the briefing when `first-game + zero-calls` (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:72, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:181).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The row may be shorthand that intended `first-game` to apply only to the house-rules sub-block.
findings.what_would_confirm: Clarify the render condition as `numbers_called_count === 0`, with `isFirstGameOfSession` controlling only the rules block.

findings.description: The promise that the display TV remains visually unchanged is unproven because the pack does not include `display-ui.tsx`. Moving inline rules JSX into `{ icon, text }` data could silently alter wording, spacing, line breaks, emphasis, or semantics on the public display.
findings.evidence: The non-goals require no display layout or house-rules wording change, while the proposal changes `renderHouseRulesPanel` to consume `HOUSE_RULES` from a shared constant (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:30, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:33, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:121).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The current display panel may already be simple icon-plus-text rows that can be represented losslessly by the proposed shape.
findings.what_would_confirm: Include or inspect `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:471` and compare before/after display screenshots.

findings.description: The spec assumes `stage_sequence` values are already human display labels, but the repo has shared win-stage helpers and prize keys that may use internal enum values. If this assumption is wrong, the host briefing can show raw values instead of matching the display ladder.
findings.evidence: The prize ladder section says to render stage labels verbatim and avoid a formatting helper, while the risk table still calls out `formatStageLabel` availability as something to confirm (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:115, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:194).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The database may store exactly `Line`, `Two Lines`, and `Full House` in `stage_sequence`.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check `src/types/database.ts`, `src/lib/win-stages.ts`, and the existing display prize ladder implementation before locking this approach.

findings.description: The vertical-fit plan targets the live-call state, but the first-game briefing is much taller and is also required to fit above the fold. Without explicit briefing sizing or overflow behavior, the new zero-call screen may still clip the primary controls on iPhone-class viewports.
findings.evidence: The verification requires full game 1 briefing plus controls without scrolling, but the spacing diet target is specifically a game with at least one number called (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:141, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:150).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: A compact briefing implementation may fit in practice, but that is not established by the proposed spacing table.
findings.what_would_confirm: Add an explicit mobile layout constraint for the briefing and verify with an iPhone 14 Pro viewport screenshot before merge.

findings.description: The colour-name fallback undermines the accessibility requirement if existing `background_colour` values are not strict `#rrggbb` hex. In that case the host gets only a dot even though the spec says the word is needed because the host is colour-blind.
findings.evidence: The header requirement says to show a colour name in words for accessibility, but invalid hex returns an empty string and falls back to the dot only (tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:103, tasks/host-controller-tweaks/SPEC.md:109).
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The schema or admin UI may already enforce valid six-digit hex values for every game background colour.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify the `games.background_colour` schema, seeds, and admin form validation; otherwise define an `Unknown colour` fallback or normalize accepted colour formats.

empty_categories: No direct server-action, RLS, or privileged-write changes are proposed in the changed pack.
No middleware/proxy broadening is proposed, so the public display/player auth-performance gotcha appears preserved.
No destructive migration, schema change, or new environment variable appears in the diff.
